summary: A symmetrical defensive structure featuring a central tower flanked by two shorter corner towers, with a stepped foundation base and white stone perimeter wall. The building combines brick and stone materials with a flat roofline topped by dark wood overhangs. Multiple windows and glass panes are integrated into the central tower section.

shape.overall_form: Cross or plus-sign shape with central tower and four corner bastions
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along primary axis; asymmetrical tower heights create visual hierarchy

elements: two tall corner towers with flat roofs
stepped foundation base with white stone blocks
rebuild_hints: Build stepped foundation first using white stone blocks in concentric rectangles
Construct central tower core with brick, approximately 4 blocks wide
Add two taller corner towers (left and right) with brick walls
Place glass windows in central tower section at mid-height
Add horizontal stonebrick trim bands between brick layers for visual separation
Install dark wood slabs as roof overhangs on all tower tops
Create perimeter fence using white stone blocks around entire footprint
Ensure flat roofline across all towers with consistent height
Add small decorative towers or chimneys on roof corners
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room configuration not visible
Precise block count for foundation steps uncertain from angles shown
Whether corner structures are solid or hollow towers
Exact glass window pattern and pane arrangement in central tower
Possible interior floors and their construction materials
Whether the perimeter fence extends fully or is decorative only
